rules.description: On curved or wrapped Hex boards, evaluate connection by explicit target zones and graph neighbors, not by screen distance or ordinary flat-board edges.
Robots must not occupy the opponent's marked camp only to block a target side; choose legal cut points outside forbidden camp cells.
Virtual connection ideas still transfer to topology boards when they are computed on the real graph: shared empty connectors and resilient bridge pairs should improve move ranking.
For 3D, wrapped, sphere, trefoil, and marked-zone Hex boards, shortest-path and cut-corridor analysis must use declared graph edges and target-zone predicates instead of flat-board coordinates.
Prefer moves that merge multiple friendly graph components or connect both target-front distance maps, because topology boards can make screen-near groups graph-far and screen-far groups graph-near.
